findings.evidence: Roadmap lines 200-205 say `IsSemigroupGroupPD` is this predicate on `ℝ≥0 × V` via the product `StarAddMonoid`; `code/TauCeti/Analysis/PositiveDefinite/Basic.lean:79` defines `TauCeti.IsPositiveDefinite`, while this PR uses `IsPositiveDefiniteKernel` directly at lines 63-64.
findings.fix: Define/place `IsSemigroupGroupPD` as the specialization of `IsPositiveDefinite` on `ℝ≥0 × V` with the intended product involution, and put only kernel bridge lemmas in the kernel-correspondence layer if needed.
findings.issue: `IsSemigroupGroupPD` is introduced as a separate `IsPositiveDefiniteKernel` predicate, bypassing the canonical generic `IsPositiveDefinite` object already in `PositiveDefinite.Basic`.

summary: The new object is placed at the kernel layer instead of as the roadmap-specified specialization of the existing generic positive-definite-function API.
